key_strengths: FAA-enforced sole-source positioning on 33,000+ niche SKUs — legally enforced near-monopoly on aftermarket parts
54% EBITDA As Defined margins sustained for 20+ years — structural, not cyclical
Asset-light model: capex only 1.9% of revenue, $2.4B+ annual free cash flow
30-year founder playbook institutionalized across multiple CEOs with consistent capital allocation discipline
Diversified revenue mix (32% commercial aftermarket, 28% OEM, 40% defense) with non-discretionary demand drivers
key_risks: ~$25B gross debt at ~5x net leverage with only 3.1–3.4x EBITDA interest coverage — amplifies any aviation downturn into an outsized equity decline
Price margin of safety near zero at $1,258 — stock trades at or above the top of the $1,050–1,200 base-case intrinsic value range
DoD regulatory pricing scrutiny on sole-source defense aftermarket parts — recurring political risk with potential structural margin impact
CEO transition (October 2025): Mike Lisman untested as CEO through a downturn
Acquisition pipeline dependency — organic growth supplemented by 1–3 deals per year; pipeline drying up or prices becoming uneconomic would impair the compounding model
red_flags: No price margin of safety at current levels — downside/upside asymmetry modestly negative
EBITDA-to-interest coverage of 3.1–3.4x is structurally thin; a 20% EBITDA decline pushes coverage below 2.5x
$90/share special dividend funded entirely by new debt — gross debt rose from ~$21B to ~$25B in a single step
GAAP earnings substantially below adjusted figures — intangible amortization is a real economic cost of the acquisition model
COVID-2020 demonstrated 55–60% stock drawdown even as the business remained cash flow positive — leverage amplifies stock volatility beyond business fundamentals
buy_triggers: Stock retreats to $950–1,050 — entering the base-case intrinsic value range with a genuine margin of safety
Aviation demand shock drives TDG below $900, creating a crisis-entry opportunity for a 5%+ position
Net leverage deleverages to below 4.5x EBITDA ahead of schedule, materially reducing tail-risk amplification
sell_triggers: Regulatory action mandates cost-plus pricing on sole-source defense aftermarket parts — thesis structurally broken, exit regardless of price
Net debt/EBITDA rises above 7x for reasons other than a specific large acquisition with a clear integration path
Stock re-rates to 25x+ forward EV/EBITDA without corresponding step-change in earnings power or leverage reduction — trim to starter position
valuation_summary: At ~$1,258/share, TDG trades at ~18x forward EV/EBITDA and ~40x owner earnings, placing it at or slightly above the top of the $1,050–1,200 base-case intrinsic value range; the stock is fairly valued on continuation of the proven playbook but offers no price margin of safety — bear-case downside is 35–45% and realistic upside is 20–25%, yielding modestly negative asymmetry at current levels.
source_summary: Analysis based on TransDigm FY2025 10-K, Q1–Q4 FY2025 and Q1 FY2026 earnings releases and transcripts, GuruFocus ROIC and valuation data, MacroTrends historical financials, MarketBeat/TipRanks analyst consensus, and DoD public oversight reports — all sourced in March 2026 with no material staleness.
